title: Flaky Pastry Tunisian Almonds Galette
description: A brimming, silky Tunisian-inspired dish featuring almond extract and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients, perfect for brunch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, confectioners' sugar, and salt.
Add the cold unsalted butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg, almond extract, and water until well combined.
Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until the dough comes together in a ball.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a copper mug or a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a small bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar, ground almonds, and a pinch of salt.
Spread the almond mixture evenly over the center of the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Crimp the edges with a fork to prevent the filling from escaping during baking.
Bake the galette in a preheated oven at 37518 Fahrenheit for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove the galette from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
Serve with a dollop of whipped cream or a sprinkle of chopped almonds if desired.
